post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 179: Starcraft Mafia: Remastered
Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
    • 1.5: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.6: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.7: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.8: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.19: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm by posting, in bold, the name of a unit that was in the Starcraft 2 standard multiplayer but was not in the Starcraft Brood War standard multiplayer. A unit from Brood War and a unit from Starcraft 2 are considered different if they have different names or if the unit/building which produces them is different. You may not use a unit to confirm if it has already been used to confirm by another player.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and Vote: fDAs is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, Vote:fdas, Vote: fads, Vote: fdsa, and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.10: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.11: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Since we use Blizzard time, each day will be 336 hours (2 weeks) rather than 3 weeks. The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 3.5: The night deadline will be 120 hours after night begins.
    • 3.6: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.7: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.8: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.11: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.12: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.13: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, people can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (fdas) am the moderator of Mafia Game 179
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Mechanics
Overview
Every player will be a unit from Starcraft Brood War. Terran = town Zerg = mafia. Each player will have the same stats as the unit from Starcraft Brood War as of 1.18. All units are considered to be ground units for this game. You can see the stats at https://liquipedia.net/starcraft/Units or on http://classic.battle.net/scc/ . A spreadsheet of all units can be seen here. You can message me at any time to ask for the stats of any unit or if you have questions about anything. Each night a player can attack and deal damage to another player who may be able to attack you back if his range is greater than or equal to yours. Certain units may also have abilities which cost energy that they can use.


Taking damage
Every day, the person who receives a majority of the votes will receive 100 damage (ignores armor). When a person’s total health reaches 0, the person will be dead and out of the game.
During the night, players can choose to attack another player. They will attack that person once. If the other player has equal range to your or greater range then you then they can attack back.
”liquipedia” wrote:
Damage in StarCraft is applied using basic mathematical equations. It begins with whether the attack hit or missed, if it does hit, the engine calculates the damage as follows: how much damage the attacking unit does, subtracts the armor of the receiving unit, then multiplies by the size vs. damage type modifier. The only exception to the final rule is Shields. Shields instead take full damage from all sources, regardless of the unit's size or damage type modifiers but damage is still reduced by shield armor.

In essence, damage = (attack-armor)*multiplier. Damage will be rounded up.

Units that do normal damage will deal 100% damage to all units.
Units that do concussive damage will deal 100% damage to small units, 50% damage to medium units, and 25% damage to large units.
Units that do explosive damage will deal 50% damage to small units, 75% damage to medium units, and 100% damage to large units.



Health/Energy Regeneration

If you are a zerg unit then at the end of each night you will regain 10 health. Your current health cannot exceed your maximum health.
Units regain 20 energy at the end of each night.
People will not be able to see exactly how much health/shields they currently have, but they can see the color of their health bar. Green is between 6/7 and 7/7, yellow is between 4/7 and 6/7, orange is between 2/7 and 4/7, red is between 0 and 2/7.
People can see how much energy they currently have.


Sample Role PM
Please note that pictures will be taken from https://starcraft.fandom.com/wiki/Category:StarCraft_Terran_units
(be sure to use the SCR picture (the hd one)
Quote:
You are a Marine (Terran).



You win when all remaining players are Terran (Town).


Statistics:

Armor: 0
Damage: 6
Damage Type: Normal
Range: 4
Size: Small

post_rendered:
In the office of the Colonial Magistrate, March 2500

fdas was seated at his desk, reviewing the various documents reporting on the state of the colony. He governed a small fringe world that up until recently was ruled by the Confederacy of Man. The events of the past few months had shaken the sector. Tarsonis, the mightiest of the terran worlds, had fallen to the zerg invasion, and with the planet's fall, the Confederacy was dead. The tide of the alien menace seemed virtually unstoppable, and if the zerg were to devote their full attention to this planet, the planet would no doubt fall. The only hope was that they were too distracted to send a significant force here. Still, that didn't mean that they could be idle. fdas was currently organizing military forces for the defense of the colony. Now that they were without central authority, they had to fend for themselves. Though, he has just finished watching the UNN broadcast in which Arcturus Mengsk was crowned Emperor of the Terran Dominion, a new polity that sought to unite the shattered fragments of the Confederacy. Mengsk had implored the rest of the Terran worlds to join him, but fdas was still apprehensive about such a move. He sighed, though he was still holding out on making a decision it seemed that he might not have much of a choice, the colony would be vulnerable on its own.

Suddenly, a sound was heard in the office. fdas turned around, only to see the terrifying visage of a zerg. His eyes widened in shock but before he could react, the zerg struck.

The next morning, fdas's body was found, lying in a pool of blood. His body was brutally mutilated. It was clear that he had been killed by zerg infiltrators. The colonists flew into a frenzy. They had lost their leadership, and now they had to root out the zerg infestation by themselves.

fdas (Colonial Magistrate) was killed Night 0.

The tide of the alien menace seemed virtually unstoppable, and if the zerg were to devote their full attention to this planet, the planet would no doubt fall. The only hope was that they were too distracted to send a significant force here. Still, that didn't mean that they could be idle. fdas was currently organizing military forces for the defense of the colony. Now that they were without central authority, they had to fend for themselves. Though, he has just finished watching the UNN broadcast in which Arcturus Mengsk was crowned Emperor of the Terran Dominion, a new polity that sought to unite the shattered fragments of the Confederacy. Mengsk had implored the rest of the Terran worlds to join him, but fdas was still apprehensive about such a move. He sighed, though he was still holding out on making a decision it seemed that he might not have much of a choice, the colony would be vulnerable on its own.

Suddenly, a sound was heard in the office. fdas turned around, only to see the terrifying visage of a zerg. His eyes widened in shock but before he could react, the zerg struck.

The next morning, fdas's body was found, lying in a pool of blood. His body was brutally mutilated. It was clear that he had been killed by zerg infiltrators. The colonists flew into a frenzy. They had lost their leadership, and now they had to root out the zerg infestation by themselves.

[color=#0f0]fdas (Colonial Magistrate)[/color] was killed Night 0.

Health/Energy Regeneration

If you are a zerg unit then at the end of each night you will regain 10 health. Your current health cannot exceed your maximum health.
Units regain 20 energy at the end of each night.
People will not be able to see exactly how much health/shields they currently have, but they can see the color of their health bar. Green is between 6/7 and 7/7, yellow is between 4/7 and 6/7, orange is between 2/7 and 4/7, red is between 0 and 2/7.
People can see how much energy they currently have.

post_rendered:
Looking back on the game in retrospect, there were a few issues with the setup. Firstly, I had counted on the dark archon to prevent follow the cop, but that sort of didn't pan out. If Madelyn had played correctly, chess would have straight up not died. On the other hand, cult was very well balanced since their recruitment was limited by energy. So, the issue of mafia lacking kill power and roleblock capability could be solved by making the game 17p and adding a mafia member who is a Queen
You are a Queen (Zerg).



During the night you may talk with your partners here.


You win when all remaining players are Zerg (Mafia).

Parasite:

Target unit is planted with a parasite. You will gain knowledge of who the target visited each night (parasite remains active until removed).

Target unit and all units which attempt to visit the target this night will be unable to carry out their action this night.

Energy: 200
.

In addition, I realize that mafia are quite disadvantaged in that they cannot roleclaim effectively, and a mass claim would break the game *If town was smart. But town was not able to take advantage of the massclaim in this game. I had thought about this, and initially I was going to put two battlecruisers in the game to screw with this kind of claim analysis, but I ran out of space in the game after making one of each role. I was also going to put multiple goliaths, but there wasn't room either.

My solution to making a more balanced Starcraft Brood War Mafia game, by making the setup semi-open.
The Setup
17 players
1 guardian
1 ultralisk
1 defiler
1 queen
1 dark archon
12 Terran, to be determined randomly

Method of determining town roles.
Each role is assigned a weight in points and a probability of being chosen.
Points
Science Vessel: 6 points 5%
Battlecruiser: 5 points 5%
Medic: 4 points 10%
SCV: 4 points 10%
Vulture: 3 points 5%
Goliath: 2 points 55%
Ghost: 2 points 10%


Town is given 24 points. For each player, their role is randomly assigned and the points are deducted from the total. If there are not enough points left then the role can no longer be given. Once the number of points runs out everyone else is made a marine. Only one science vessel is allowed.

But town was not able to take advantage of the massclaim in this game. I had thought about this, and initially I was going to put two battlecruisers in the game to screw with this kind of claim analysis, but I ran out of space in the game after making one of each role. I was also going to put multiple goliaths, but there wasn't room either.

My solution to making a more balanced Starcraft Brood War Mafia game, by making the setup semi-open.
[hide="The Setup"]17 players
[color=#f00]1 guardian
1 ultralisk
1 defiler
1 queen[/color]
[color=#9a00ff]1 dark archon[/color]
[color=#0f0]12 Terran, to be determined randomly[/color]

Method of determining town roles.
Each role is assigned a weight in points and a probability of being chosen.
[hide=Points]Science Vessel: 6 points 5%
Battlecruiser: 5 points 5%
Medic: 4 points 10%
SCV: 4 points 10%
Vulture: 3 points 5%
Goliath: 2 points 55%
Ghost: 2 points 10%
[/hide]

Town is given 24 points. For each player, their role is randomly assigned and the points are deducted from the total. If there are not enough points left then the role can no longer be given. Once the number of points runs out everyone else is made a marine. Only one science vessel is allowed.
